questions.q: Who were Subhadra's famous brothers?
questions.explanation: Subhadra was the sister of Krishna and Balarama, the foremost princes of the Yadavas.

questions.q: Whom did Subhadra marry?
questions.explanation: Subhadra became the wife of Arjuna, the third Pandava, after he carried her off with her consent.

questions.q: Who was Subhadra's celebrated son?
questions.explanation: Subhadra bore Abhimanyu, the young hero who broke into the Chakravyuha on the thirteenth day of the war.

questions.q: To whom did Balarama wish to give Subhadra in marriage?
questions.explanation: Balarama had promised Subhadra to his favorite pupil Duryodhana, a plan Krishna quietly undid.

questions.q: What did Subhadra famously do during her carrying-off from Dwarka?
options: She disguised herself as a soldier
She took the reins and drove the chariot herself
She sent a decoy maid in her place
She signalled the Yadava guards for rescue
questions.explanation: In the popular retelling she took the reins herself so no one could call it a forcible abduction; Vyasa's epic instead has Arjuna carry her off, with Krishna defending the act afterward.

questions.q: What did Abhimanyu absorb while still in Subhadra's womb?
options: The speech of birds and beasts
questions.explanation: The unborn Abhimanyu listened as the entry into the wheel formation was described, knowledge that shaped his fate.

questions.q: Who was Subhadra's father?
questions.explanation: Subhadra was the daughter of Vasudeva, which made her a sister of Krishna and Balarama.

questions.q: Who urged Arjuna to carry Subhadra off rather than wait for a swayamvara?
questions.explanation: Krishna advised that a swayamvara's outcome was uncertain and that heroic seizure of a willing bride was praised for kshatriyas.

questions.q: Who became Subhadra's daughter-in-law?
questions.explanation: Uttara, the princess of Virata, married Subhadra's son Abhimanyu.

questions.q: Which grandson of Subhadra carried on the Kuru line after the war?
questions.explanation: Parikshit, son of Abhimanyu and Uttara, was the sole surviving heir who continued the dynasty.

questions.q: Where did Arjuna first see Subhadra?
options: At a festival on Mount Raivataka near Dwarka
questions.explanation: Arjuna beheld Subhadra during the great festival on Raivataka and was instantly captivated.

questions.q: Why was Arjuna travelling near Dwarka when he met Subhadra?
options: He was conquering the west for the Rajasuya
He was fleeing the house of lac
He was on a twelve-year pilgrimage for breaching the brothers' pact over Draupadi
He had come to invite Krishna to Indraprastha
questions.explanation: Arjuna had entered the chamber where Yudhishthira sat with Draupadi and accepted a twelve-year pilgrimage of expiation, which brought him to the western coast.

questions.q: In what dress did Subhadra first approach Draupadi at Indraprastha?
questions.explanation: At Krishna's prompting Subhadra came to Draupadi dressed as a humble cowherd woman and won her over by presenting herself as a serving companion.

questions.q: Why did Subhadra's hand on the chariot reins matter so much?
options: It proved her horsemanship outshone Arjuna's
It fulfilled a childhood vow to her mother
It let the couple outrun the pursuing Yadavas
It showed she eloped by her own free choice, not by force
questions.explanation: With Subhadra herself driving, the departure could not be condemned as a forcible abduction, for her consent was plain to all.

questions.q: How did Krishna soothe Balarama's anger over the elopement?
options: He swore an oath that Arjuna would be punished
He offered to fight Arjuna himself
He argued no groom outshone Arjuna and that heroic seizure was honored custom
He produced a letter of consent written by Subhadra
questions.explanation: Krishna reasoned that Arjuna was the worthiest possible match and that carrying off a bride was a recognized practice for heroes, so the marriage should be celebrated.

questions.q: What kinship already linked Subhadra and Arjuna before their marriage?
options: They were cousins, since her father Vasudeva was Kunti's brother
They were siblings by adoption under Shurasena
She was the niece of his stepmother Madri
They were linked only through Draupadi's family
questions.explanation: Kunti was Vasudeva's sister, so Subhadra and Arjuna were cross-cousins, a match tradition considered fitting.

questions.q: Which part of the Chakravyuha lore never reached the child in Subhadra's womb?
options: The way out of the wheel
The hour to strike the centre
questions.explanation: The narration broke off before the escape was described, so Abhimanyu knew how to enter the formation but never how to leave it.

questions.q: Where were the wedding rites of Arjuna and Subhadra celebrated?
questions.explanation: Once Balarama was reconciled, Arjuna was brought back and married Subhadra with full honors at Dwarka.

questions.q: What political bond did Subhadra's marriage seal?
options: The alliance of the Yadavas with the Pandavas
The truce between the Kurus and Panchalas
The pact binding Matsya to Madra
The league of Magadha and Chedi
questions.explanation: The marriage tied Krishna's Yadava clan to the Pandava cause by blood, an alliance that endured through the war.

questions.q: In which city was Subhadra's son Abhimanyu born and raised?
questions.explanation: Subhadra came to Indraprastha with Arjuna, and Abhimanyu was born there during the Pandavas' golden years.

questions.q: How did the Yadava warriors first react to news of Subhadra's elopement?
options: They rejoiced and prepared the wedding
They sprang up armed for pursuit until counsel calmed them
They sent envoys to Hastinapura for arbitration
They locked the city gates and went into mourning
questions.explanation: The Yadavas gathered in arms to chase Arjuna, but Krishna's reasoning and Balarama's eventual consent turned pursuit into celebration.

questions.q: In the popular telling, who was narrating the Chakravyuha when the pregnant Subhadra drifted asleep?
questions.explanation: Most retellings have Arjuna describing the formation to Subhadra when sleep overtook her, though some variants make Krishna the narrator.

questions.q: With which goddess is Subhadra identified in tradition?
questions.explanation: Tradition identifies Subhadra with Yogamaya or Ekanamsha, the divine power born among the Yadavas, while Shri is identified with Draupadi.

questions.q: In the Jagannath temple at Puri, where does Subhadra stand?
options: At Jagannath's feet on a lotus
Behind the brothers on a raised seat
Between her brothers Jagannath and Balabhadra
Facing the triad from across the hall
questions.explanation: At Puri, Subhadra is worshipped on the central seat between Jagannath, a form of Krishna, and Balabhadra, a form of Balarama.

questions.q: Subhadra's son Abhimanyu was held to be the incarnation of whom?
options: A Vasu freed from an ancient curse
An aspect of Agni the fire god
Varchas, son of the moon god Soma
questions.explanation: The Adi Parva says Soma lent his son Varchas to be born as Abhimanyu on condition that he return after sixteen years.

questions.q: In a well-known variant retelling, what guise let Arjuna live close to Subhadra in Dwarka?
options: A wandering renunciate monk sheltering for the rains
A blind minstrel of the court
questions.explanation: Later and regional retellings have Arjuna pose as a yati hosted for the monsoon with Subhadra attending him, whereas Vyasa's epic has him attend the Raivataka festival openly.

questions.q: When the Pandavas left on their final journey, what duty fell to Subhadra?
options: To accompany them into the Himalayas
To watch over young Parikshit and Vajra in the kingdom
To return to Dwarka as its regent
questions.explanation: In the Mahaprasthanika Parva, Yudhishthira asked Subhadra to remain and care for Parikshit at Hastinapura and to treat Vajra as her own son.

questions.q: Who is most commonly named as Subhadra's mother?
questions.explanation: Most traditions make Subhadra the daughter of Rohini, Balarama's mother, though a few later texts name Devaki.

questions.q: Which section of the Adi Parva is named for Subhadra's carrying-away?
questions.explanation: The episode is told in the Subhadra-harana Parva, literally the book of the seizing of Subhadra.

questions.q: Who consoled Subhadra after Abhimanyu fell inside the Chakravyuha?
options: Vyasa, reciting the doctrine of time
Krishna, with words on a hero's deathless glory
questions.explanation: In the Drona Parva, Krishna comforted the grieving Subhadra, Draupadi, and Uttara, assuring them of the slain hero's blessed destiny.
